finding.summary: Track capture is entirely silent (silence_fraction = 1.0). Sample peak, RMS, and integrated LUFS all sit at the noise floor, so every level-based statistic is diluted by silence and not interpretable. The non-zero spectrum band levels (-18 to -30 dB) are inconsistent with the -120 dB sample peak and cannot be reconciled from a single isolated capture.
finding.probable_cause: No audio content is reaching the track: the source may be muted, disconnected, routed elsewhere, or the capture window contains no material (dead air). The mismatched spectrum readings likely reflect analyzer noise-floor or capture-window artifacts rather than real signal.
finding.confidence_reason: Confidence is capped at low because audio.silence_fraction is 1.000.

evidence_refs.description: 100% of the capture window is silence, disqualifying level-based diagnosis.
Sample peak at the noise floor; no real signal present.
RMS at the noise floor, consistent with silence.
Integrated loudness is meaningless when the window is entirely silent.
Crest factor of 12 dB on silence is an artifact, not a usable dynamic metric.

proposal.reason: audio.silence_fraction is 1.0, which exceeds the 0.75 confidence floor. With no verified signal, no single-track parameter change can be justified. Recommend verifying that the source is unmuted, routed to this track, and that the capture window actually contains audio before re-measuring.
